Title: Anthocyanins attenuate endothelial dysfunction through regulation of uncoupling of nitric oxide synthase in aged rats

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Anthocyanin/mulberry treatment increases activating Ser1177 phosphorylation, decreases inhibitory Thr495 phosphorylation and acetylation, thereby restoring eNOS activity and NO production
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Serum and aorta in rats; endothelial cells in HUVEC culture
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports endothelial function and vascular homeostasis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Western blot of total and phospho-eNOS; immunofluorescence for p-eNOS Ser1177 in aorta; eNOS immunoprecipitation and acetyl-lysine blot; NO quantification by Griess and DAF-2DA; peroxynitrite/nitrotyrosine assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Aging in rats; d-galactose-induced senescence in HUVECs; anthocyanin-rich mulberry extract; cyanidin-3-rutinoside; cyanidin-3-glucoside
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Aging and d-gal-induced senescence suppressed eNOS activation and increased eNOS acetylation. 2. Anthocyanins/mulberry restored activating phosphorylation of eNOS and reduced acetylation. 3. Improved eNOS status correlated with higher NO and lower peroxynitrite/nitrotyrosine, indicating reduced eNOS uncoupling.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: eNOS is the principal mechanistic effector measured; paper focuses on post-translational regulation rather than transcriptional changes
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: eNOS total protein unchanged; activation state altered by phosphorylation and acetylation

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein deacetylation; positive regulation of eNOS activity
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Deacetylates eNOS and is associated with increased eNOS activity; restored SIRT1 expression/activity correlates with increased eNOS Ser1177 phosphorylation and NO bioavailability
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Nitric oxide biosynthesis and endothelial redox homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Anthocyanin/mulberry treatment restores SIRT1 expression and activity, reduces eNOS acetylation, increases NO production, and attenuates endothelial senescence/oxidative stress
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator of NO bioavailability through eNOS deacetylation in aging/senescent endothelium
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Maintains vascular endothelial function and limits endothelial dysfunction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Western blot for SIRT1; sirtuin activity assay; NAD+/NADH ratio measurement; eNOS immunoprecipitation followed by acetyl-lysine and SIRT1 immunoblotting; NO measurements; in vivo rat and in vitro HUVEC senescence models
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Aging in 80-week-old rats; d-galactose-induced senescence in HUVECs; anthocyanin-rich mulberry extract; cyanidin-3-rutinoside; cyanidin-3-glucoside
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Anthocyanin-rich mulberry extract; cyanidin-3-rutinoside; cyanidin-3-glucoside; aging/d-galactose status; NAD+/NADH ratio
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in aging rat aorta and d-gal-treated HUVECs; restored by anthocyanin/mulberry treatment
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports use of anthocyanin-rich functional foods or interventions that enhance SIRT1-eNOS axis for vascular health; not a crop breeding target in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper shows SIRT1 as mechanistic mediator of anthocyanin effect, but no direct genetic manipulation of SIRT1 was performed in this study
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Aging and d-gal-induced senescence reduced SIRT1 expression/activity and NAD+/NADH ratio. 2. Anthocyanins and mulberry extract restored SIRT1 levels and activity. 3. Restored SIRT1 was associated with lower eNOS acetylation, greater eNOS activity, higher NO, and reduced endothelial dysfunction.
